_comment: Read-side registry of cross-run-by-design channels (read-side metadata only — write-side namespace policy is independent). runs_reader.read_jsonl(scope='cross-run-by-design') requires that cross_run_channel= argument names an entry here AND that the path is declared under that entry. Lockstep with lifecycle-init.sh STALE_ARTIFACTS is enforced by the cross_run_channel_exemption_pairing coherence rule (.claude/patterns/template-coherence-rules.json). To add a new channel: in the PR that creates the underlying .runs/*.jsonl file, append an entry here with paths[], rationale, owner, and (when the path is intentionally per-skill-cleared) transient_cross_state: true; also add the path to lifecycle-init.sh STALE_ARTIFACTS exemption mechanism. New channels MUST be atomic with the file that owns them — no forward-declaration to avoid registry/file drift.

fix-ledger.rationale: Authoritative append-only ledger of agent + lead fixes across runs. Persists across lifecycle-init (not in STALE_ARTIFACTS). Used by recurrence-detector.py for cross-run composite-identity grouping and by check-cross-artifact-consistency.py for run-id-scoped counts.

recurrence-candidates.rationale: Phase B recurrence-candidate snapshots; explicitly cross-run for trend analysis.

agent-spawn-log.rationale: Append-only log of agent spawn events; cross-run for lineage checks (e.g., skill-agent-gate.sh lineage validation).

lead-deviation-log.rationale: Append-only log of prose-gate violations across runs. Cross-run-by-design: enumerate-pending-retrospective-findings.py 6th candidate source uses entries from prior runs to surface untriaged violations. Preserved by lifecycle-init.sh:287-294. Closes PR-#1444 omission (registry was missing this entry; #1431).

lead-deviation-log-write-failures.rationale: Append-only log of failed deviation-log writes. Cross-run for visibility — silent appender failures otherwise undetectable. Read by enumerate-pending-retrospective-findings.py 7th candidate source as HIGH-confidence log-write-failure candidates. Closes #1431 reliability gap.
